paper_title: FMIP: Joint Continuous-Integer Flow For Mixed-Integer Linear Programming

research_question.summary: Jointly model integer and continuous variables in MILP solution generation so learning-based heuristics can better guide downstream solvers.

high_level_algorithmic_idea.summary: Use a conditional flow matching framework to generate complete MILP solutions over both integer and continuous variables, then apply holistic guidance based on objective values and constraint violations during sampling.

training_label_dataset.summary: The method learns from MILP solution distributions and is evaluated on eight standard MILP benchmarks, though the exact benchmark names are not fully recovered in this quick scan.

network_structure.summary: A backbone-agnostic joint continuous-integer generative framework centered on flow-based solution modeling rather than a single fixed graph encoder.

network_output.summary: A complete candidate MILP solution containing both integer and continuous variables.

loss_function.summary: Conditional flow matching is explicitly named as the training paradigm, but the exact full loss formulation is not fully extracted in this first pass.

inference_policy.summary: Iteratively sample and refine a full solution trajectory with holistic guidance from objective feedback and constraint violations, then use the result with downstream solvers.

novel_contribution.summary: The first generative MILP framework that jointly models integer and continuous variables, enabling a stronger guidance mechanism and better compatibility with diverse backbones and solvers.

unresolved_questions: The exact benchmark families and full training loss need a slower read.
The way FMIP plugs into specific downstream solvers should be verified in the experiments section.
